title: issue template: add platform
body:
Extend the issue template to make it (more) obvious to which platform a
given issue or feature request relates to.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

@mheon PTAL

Do I interpret `/kind` correctly to apply the specified label?

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
I think it will only look in labels prefixes `kind/` - so we'd need `kind/macos` and similar labels for this.
I think it is better to just read OsArch from the podman info output, we could create a job to automatically label issues based on this value

> I think it is better to just read OsArch from the podman info output, we could create a job to automatically label issues based on this value

Feel free to open a PR :)
Can we close this PR, or wait to see if @Luap99 PR works?
If we decide to go with this, changes LGTM.  I'll let others make the call on going with this or #13050
We can reopen in case https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13050 doesn't work as expected.
